SEARCH

Class GameEngine

java.lang.Object
GameEngine
public class GameEngine extends Object
Central coordinator for game state, room navigation, and command handling.

Responsibilities include building the world graph, maintaining the current room and a stack for the back command, delegating user input to the Parser, and driving output through the UserInterface.

Version:
2026.02.17
Author:
Barnabe Jouanard

    Description
    private Room
    aCurrentRoom
    The room the player currently occupies.
    private UserInterface
    aGui
    User-facing output and optional room imagery.
    private Parser

    Converts raw input lines into Command objects.
    private Stack<Room>
    aPreviousRooms
    Rooms visited along the current path; used to support back.

    Description

  • Method Summary

    Modifier and Type
    Method
    Description
    private void
    createRooms()
    Creates every Room, connects exits, places Items, and sets the player's starting location.
    private void
    eat()
    Placeholder response for the eat command.
    private void
    endGame()
    Ends the session by printing a farewell line and disabling further input.
    private void
    goBack()
    Pops the most recent room from the history stack and restores it as current, then refreshes the textual and graphical view.

    Moves the player in the direction given as the command's second word.

    Parses and executes one player command line.

    look()
    Re-prints the full description of the room the player is standing in.
    private void
    printHelp()
    Prints short narrative context and the list of valid command words.
    private void
    printLocationInfo()
    Prints the long description of the current room (including exits and items).
    private void
    printWelcome()
    Prints the welcome banner, initial location text, and the starting room image when one is configured.
    void
    setGUI(UserInterface pUserInterface)
    Attaches the user interface used for all player feedback.

  • Field Details

    • aCurrentRoom

      private Room aCurrentRoom
      The room the player currently occupies.

    • aPreviousRooms

      private Stack<Room> aPreviousRooms
      Rooms visited along the current path; used to support back.

    • aParser

      private Parser aParser
      Converts raw input lines into Command objects.

    • aGui

      private UserInterface aGui
      User-facing output and optional room imagery.

  • Constructor Details

    • GameEngine

      public GameEngine()
      Builds the default map and creates the command parser.

      The GUI is not set here; call setGUI(UserInterface) after the interface exists so welcome text and images can be shown.

  • Method Details

    • setGUI

      public void setGUI(UserInterface pUserInterface)
      Attaches the user interface used for all player feedback.
      Parameters:
      pUserInterface - the Swing UI bound to this engine; must not be null for normal play

    • createRooms

      private void createRooms()
      Creates every Room, connects exits, places Items, and sets the player's starting location.

    • printWelcome

      private void printWelcome()
      Prints the welcome banner, initial location text, and the starting room image when one is configured.

    • goRoom

      private void goRoom(Command pCommand)
      Moves the player in the direction given as the command's second word.

      If the direction is missing, or there is no exit, an explanatory message is printed. Otherwise the current room is pushed onto the history stack before changing rooms, then the new description and image are shown.

      Parameters:
      pCommand - a go command whose second token is the direction

    • printHelp

      private void printHelp()
      Prints short narrative context and the list of valid command words.

    • printLocationInfo

      private void printLocationInfo()
      Prints the long description of the current room (including exits and items).

    • interpretCommand

      public void interpretCommand(String pCommand)
      Parses and executes one player command line.

      Recognized verbs include help, go, eat, look, back, and quit. Unknown input produces a generic error message.

      Parameters:
      pCommand - the raw text entered by the player (may be empty)

    • look

      private void look()
      Re-prints the full description of the room the player is standing in.

    • eat

      private void eat()
      Placeholder response for the eat command.

    • endGame

      private void endGame()
      Ends the session by printing a farewell line and disabling further input.

    • goBack

      private void goBack()
      Pops the most recent room from the history stack and restores it as current, then refreshes the textual and graphical view.
